title: telis aurantiaca
intro: Telis aurantiaca, also known as the Orange Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Telis aurantiaca include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and health.
intro_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Telis aurantiaca, which contains auraptene, a compound found in citrus fruits, has been shown to promote effects on T cell subsets toward Th1 (IFN-γ) and Treg (IL-10), suggesting its therapeutic value for the treatment of Th2 cells predominant diseases, including allergic diseases and cancers.
